title: Data storage & dataset API modernization
description:
Modernize data storage and dataset access for scalable deep learning workflows.

Scope:
- Evaluate HDF5 alternatives or a more robust HDF5 schema (versioned, metadata-forward).
- Add DataContainer subset/random sampling APIs and explicit subset selection.
- Handle empty/partial files gracefully during reads and preprocessing.
- Standardize metadata fields and alignment checks across specs/projections/features.
- Add tests covering empty/missing inputs and subset sampling.

title: Add parquet ROI preflight + validation support for streaming training
description:
## Context
Streaming training mode (manifest-driven) is now supported for parquet ROI bundles, but the launcher currently skips ROI duration preflight and the validation script only supports per-wav txt ROIs.

## Goal
Provide basic guardrails and a validation loop for parquet+streaming workflows.

## Scope
- Add a lightweight preflight in streaming mode that samples a configurable number of clips/segments from parquet bundles and reports duration compatibility stats for window_length.
- Extend scripts/run_birdsong_validation.py to support --streaming + --roi-format=parquet (reusing the manifest streaming dataloader).

## Acceptance Criteria
- launch script prints sampled preflight stats in streaming mode and errors if zero compatible segments are found.
- validation script can run a short training loop using parquet bundles without enumerating all wav/ROI files.
- Add/extend a unit test that exercises the sampled preflight and/or validation path on a tiny synthetic parquet ROI bundle.

title: audit skipped clips in latent parquet exports for coverage
description: Review clips skipped due missing ROI rows or no usable windows, quantify by bird/regime, and decide whether to backfill ROIs or exclude in downstream TDA.

title: Latent robustness for coarse identity (augmentations + eval)
description: Ensure AVA latents capture coarse syllable identity by being robust to small acoustic variation (amplitude/noise/time/freq perturbations) and by providing a simple evaluation harness for invariance and retrieval.
notes:
Rationale: Topology over distance images assumes a distance geometry where same-syllable tokens form coherent low-distance regions even when acoustically different.

Scope:
- Define a small set of augmentations that reflect 'acoustically different but same identity'.
- Add an invariance evaluation script (original vs augmented windows, kNN retrieval within augmented sets).
- Optionally add a consistency loss during training to encourage invariance.

Testing plan:
- Unit tests for augmentation determinism (seeded).
- Short smoke test: invariance metrics run on tests/data/test.wav without NaNs.

title: Support --roi-root/--audio-root overrides even with absolute paths in manifest
description:
## Context
The full birdsong manifest stores absolute audio_dir/roi_dir per entry. Current scripts prefer those fields and only fall back to CLI --audio-root/--roi-root when missing, which blocks relocating ROI outputs off ExFAT and blocks running ROI on cloud mounts/object-store synced directories.

## Goal
Make CLI root flags override manifest absolute paths in a predictable way (while remaining backward compatible when relative info is missing).

## Proposed Behavior
- If --audio-root is provided, resolve audio_dir as <audio_root>/<audio_dir_rel> when audio_dir_rel is present; otherwise fall back to manifest audio_dir.
- If --roi-root is provided, resolve roi_dir as <roi_root>/<audio_dir_rel> when audio_dir_rel is present; otherwise fall back to manifest roi_dir.
- If no override is provided, keep existing behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
- scripts/run_birdsong_roi.py, scripts/run_birdsong_validation.py, scripts/launch_birdsong_training.py, and scripts/report_birdsong_roi_coverage.py all honor the override behavior above.
- Add a small unit test that constructs a minimal manifest entry (with audio_dir_rel, audio_dir, roi_dir) and asserts override resolution.
- No behavior regression for medium manifest workflows.

title: Medium pilot: A/B training spectrogram params vs ROI tuning
description:
Context: ROI segmentation for medium pilot uses a tuned config (min_freq=300, spec_min_val=1.0), while the training preprocess config is still closer to the finch baseline (min_freq=400, spec_min_val=1.5).

Task: run short A/B comparisons on the medium manifest to see if aligning preprocess min_freq/spec_min_val improves validation and/or reconstructions.

Plan:
- Variant A: baseline medium pilot training config
- Variant B: min_freq=300, spec_min_val=1.0 (or 1.25)
- Keep window_length=0.03 and KL schedule fixed to isolate effect.

Deliverables:
- run report(s) with metrics + artifact paths
- recommendation for preprocess defaults going into Phase 3/4
acceptance_criteria: At least one A/B run completed and documented; recommended preprocess params recorded.

title: Make training scalable to full manifest (avoid loading all ROI into memory at init)
description:
## Context
The current full-scale birdsong manifest references ~3.8M wav files. Existing dataset code paths include loaders that read ROI text files into memory at dataset initialization, which is not feasible at full scale.

## Goal
Implement a scalable training data pipeline that can train over the full manifest by streaming ROI and audio on demand.

## Proposed Approach
- Introduce a dataset implementation that:
  - enumerates examples lazily (or via an index/parquet)
  - reads ROI for a clip on demand (from txt or bundled parquet)
  - optionally caches small computed artifacts (spectrograms or ROI parsing) with bounded size
- Ensure DDP correctness (no duplicated writes, deterministic sharding).

## Acceptance Criteria
- A new dataset/dataloader path can iterate over a large synthetic manifest (and/or a real subset) without O(N) ROI loads at init.
- Memory usage stays bounded (no proportional growth with file count).
- Existing medium-manifest training scripts still work.

title: Add bundled ROI output mode (per-directory Parquet or tar.gz)
description:
## Context
Full ROI runs create millions of per-wav ROI .txt files. This is painful on object stores and on some local filesystems (e.g., ExFAT) due to metadata/sidecar overhead.

## Goal
Support a bundled ROI output mode that produces one output artifact per manifest entry (audio directory), while keeping current per-wav .txt output available for backwards compatibility.

## Proposed Implementation
- Extend scripts/run_birdsong_roi.py with a new flag (e.g., --roi-output-format=txt|parquet|tar).
- For parquet: write a single file per audio_dir_rel containing clip_stem + onset/offset rows.
- For tar: write per-wav .txt locally, then bundle into a tar.gz and delete the per-wav .txt files.
- Update scripts/report_birdsong_roi_coverage.py to optionally read bundled outputs (at least for the chosen default format).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Can run ROI over a small manifest and produce bundled outputs with deterministic naming.
- Bundled outputs are skip-able/idempotent ("exists" implies complete).
- Coverage script can compute missing/empty stats from the bundled format for the small manifest.

title: Plan full birdsong ROI run on cloud (storage, compute, cost, output format)
description:
## Context
Local full ROI is currently blocked by ExFAT overhead (AppleDouble sidecars + 128KB minimum allocation per file) and the manifest's absolute roi_dir paths.

## Goal
Produce a concrete, provider-agnostic plan (with one recommended provider) for running ROI extraction at full scale in the cloud.

## Deliverable
- A short doc in docs/ describing:
  - Recommended provider and services (object storage + batch/array compute).
  - Data transfer plan (multi-TB audio upload) and expected timelines.
  - Compute plan: job sharding strategy, expected throughput, retry/idempotency.
  - Output format recommendation to avoid millions of tiny objects (e.g., per-directory parquet/tar) and how downstream training will consume it.
  - Rough cost ranges and main cost drivers (storage, egress, compute).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Doc is actionable enough to start an implementation sprint.
- Calls out any blockers or missing decisions explicitly.

title: Latent sequence export API (for topology / temporal analysis)
description: Add a supported way to encode long audio into time-indexed latent sequences (mu/sigma) suitable for downstream temporal-structure pipelines (e.g., distance-image + persistence in juvenile_learning_tda).
notes:
Scope:
- Export per-window latent stats over time for a clip (mu, logvar/sigma), with timestamps/window start times.
- Optionally export per-window energy/gating weights.
- Include run/model provenance (checkpoint path, git commit, config hash) when available.
- Provide both a python API and a CLI script.

Testing plan:
- Unit test: export on tests/data/test.wav produces expected shapes and monotonic timestamps.
- Integration sanity: compute a distance image between two exported sequences without NaNs.

title: Define latent-sequence output schema + file format
description: Specify the canonical latent-sequence export format (fields, shapes, dtypes) and choose output container(s) (e.g., NPZ + JSON sidecar, Parquet).
notes: Include: start_times_sec, window_length_sec, hop_length_sec, mu, logvar/sigma, optional energy weights; plus provenance metadata.

title: Fix Batch submit command override for ROI container
description:
## Context
The ROI container image has an ENTRYPOINT that runs scripts/cloud/aws/run_birdsong_roi_batch_shard.py. The submit script currently overrides the Batch command with the same python invocation, causing the shard runner to receive extra argv and fail.

## Goal
Make scripts/cloud/aws/submit_birdsong_roi_batch_array_job.py generate a payload that does not duplicate the command by default (works with the current docker/Dockerfile.roi ENTRYPOINT).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Batch jobs start successfully using the documented container image
- Unit test covers payload generation to prevent regressions

title: Medium pilot: sweep KL beta/warmup
description:
Context: medium pilot run 20260212-163833 (autoencoded-vocal-analysis-381) improved val_loss to ~4.21e3 then plateaued/slightly increased by epoch 49.

Task: run a small A/B sweep on the same manifest/config baseline to evaluate whether KL schedule changes improve validation without inducing collapse.

Suggested grid (keep others fixed):
- kl_beta: 0.5, 0.7 (baseline), 1.0
- kl_warmup_epochs: 10, 20 (baseline), 30

Deliverables:
- 2-4 additional runs (50-ish epochs or shorter if clearly worse)
- run reports under docs/runs/ with config + artifacts + results
- recommend one setting for next medium/full runs
acceptance_criteria: At least 2 KL schedule variants trained and compared on the medium manifest; docs/runs reports written; recommended KL schedule recorded.

comments.author: SamuelBrudner
comments.text: Ran 3x 51-epoch KL schedule variants on the medium manifest (MPS, fp32) and compared val_loss. Best final val_loss was kl_beta=1.0, kl_warmup_epochs=20. Report: docs/runs/20260212-182621-kl-schedule-sweep-autoencoded-vocal-analysis-s67.md. Artifacts: docs/runs/artifacts/autoencoded-vocal-analysis-s67/.

title: Implement AWS S3 upload driver + Batch array-job wrapper for ROI extraction
description:
## Context
Cloud ROI extraction requires (1) staging audio to object storage and (2) running sharded ROI jobs via a batch scheduler.

## Goal
Provide minimal, reproducible tooling in-repo to stage the manifest subset to S3 and submit an AWS Batch array job that runs scripts/run_birdsong_roi.py with sharding.

## Deliverables
- A script to upload the manifest subset to S3 (with parallelism + resume).
- A script/config to submit an AWS Batch array job (num_shards + shard_index mapping) using a container image.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tools are documented and parameterized (bucket/prefix/region/manifest path).
- Sharding is deterministic; rerunning is safe.
- No hard-coded personal paths; supports --audio-root/--roi-root overrides.
